recipes.name: Chromatic Orb
recipes.ingredients: Any item with 3 linked sockets of 3 different colors (R+G+B)
recipes.notes: Works at any item level and rarity. Off-color items (e.g. high Str/Int gear with green sockets) are most cost-efficient to vendor.

recipes.name: Jeweller's Orbs (bulk)
recipes.notes: Most 6-socket items are worth more sold to vendor for jewellers than on trade. Exception: 6-linked items — vendor those instead.

recipes.name: Orbs of Fusing (bulk)
recipes.notes: Only worthwhile if the item itself has no trade value. 6-linked whites and rares with bad mods are prime candidates.

recipes.name: Portal Scroll
recipes.notes: Scroll Fragments drop from vendors when selling items. Collect them from junk vendors early in acts.

recipes.name: Orb of Transmutation
recipes.notes: Only useful if desperately short on transmutes. In practice, transmutes are plentiful from drops.

recipes.name: Orb of Augmentation
recipes.notes: Useful to know but augmentations drop frequently enough that this is rarely needed.

recipes.name: Orb of Alteration
recipes.notes: Alteration recipe is a useful sink for excess augments. Alterations are a common early-game craft currency.

recipes.name: Blacksmith's Whetstone
recipes.notes: Vendor any white/magic/rare weapon that has been quality-maxed. More efficient than selling low-value weapons for nothing.

recipes.name: Armourer's Scrap
recipes.notes: Same principle as whetstone recipe. Any slot counts: helmet, chest, gloves, boots, shield.

recipes.name: Glassblower's Bauble
recipes.notes: Flask vendors in acts often sell magic flasks you can quality-up and vendor back for baubles.

recipes.name: Cartographer's Chisel
recipes.notes: Best chisel recipe: use Chisels on a white map, brick it, vendor. Or buy low-tier white maps and quality them with baubles first.

recipes.name: Gemcutter's Prism
recipes.ingredients: White (normal) gems totaling 40% or more combined quality
recipes.notes: Use Gemcutter's Prism on white copies of cheap gems (e.g. Clarity, Rejuvenation Totem). Combine 4× 10%q gems or 2× 20%q gems.

recipes.name: Flask (upgraded tier)
recipes.ingredients: 3 flasks of the same base type and rarity
recipes.result: 1 flask of the next higher tier (same type and rarity)
recipes.notes: Excellent early-leveling recipe. Combine 3 Small Life Flasks → Medium Life Flask, etc. Works for mana and hybrid flasks too.

recipes.name: Magic flask (reroll mods)
recipes.result: 1 magic flask with new random mods
recipes.notes: Vendor-rerolls the magic flask. Faster than looting new flasks for specific mods like Surgeon's or Experimenter's.

recipes.name: Ruby/Sapphire/Topaz Ring
recipes.notes: Ruby = fire resist, Sapphire = cold resist, Topaz = lightning resist. Essential for capping resistance while leveling.

recipes.name: Two-Stone Ring
recipes.ingredients: 1 Ruby Ring + 1 Sapphire Ring, or other elemental ring combos
recipes.notes: Ruby + Sapphire → fire/cold Two-Stone Ring. Ruby + Topaz → fire/lightning. Sapphire + Topaz → cold/lightning.

recipes.name: Warding/Grounding/Dousing/Heat flask
recipes.result: Flask with curse removal or elemental ailment removal
recipes.notes: Vendor a utility flask with a matching-color gem to add relevant ailment/curse removal suffix. Exact combos vary by flask type.

recipes.name: Unique — 3× Same Unique
recipes.ingredients: 3 copies of the same unique item
recipes.result: 1 copy of that unique with new random rolls
recipes.notes: Rerolls the unique's numeric values. Useful for chasing perfect rolls on a budget unique. Works on any rarity.

recipes.name: Chance Orb (from uniques)
recipes.notes: Most worthless uniques vendor for Orbs of Chance. Better to bulk-vendor than trash. Exception: some uniques vendor for specific currency.

recipes.name: Regal Orb (from uniques)
recipes.notes: Some unique bases vendor for Regals when unidentified. Less common in modern PoE — verify in poe.wiki before relying on this.

recipes.name: Maps (upgrade tier)
recipes.ingredients: 3 maps of the same base type and rarity
recipes.result: 1 map of the next tier (same type and rarity)
recipes.notes: Core map sustain mechanic. 3× T1 → 1× T2, etc. White maps: 3 whites → 1 white next tier. Same rules apply for magic and rare maps.
